task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the wp-config.php file being edited to include 'define( "WP_ALLOW_MULTISITE", true );'.

task_completion.description: Access the Network Setup screen in WordPress Admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress Admin dashboard open in a browser, showing the 'Network Setup' page (usually under Tools) with options for Sub-directories.

task_completion.description: Update wp-config.php and .htaccess with Multisite rules
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the addition of 'MULTISITE' and 'SUBDOMAIN_INSTALL' (set to false) to wp-config.php, and the corresponding WordPress multisite rewrite rules added to the .htaccess file.
The WordPress browser window showing the 'Network Admin' dashboard (My Sites -> Network Admin), confirming the multisite installation is active.

The 'Add New Site' form in the Network Admin dashboard filled out with Site Address 'biology', Site Title 'Biology Department', and Admin Email 'admin@example.com', or a success message showing the site was added.

task_completion.description: Verify the new sub-site is accessible
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser displaying the newly created 'Biology Department' WordPress site loaded successfully at the URL path ending in '/biology/'.

integrity.description: Agent used a real web browser to configure and verify the WordPress site
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a genuine web browser interface (like Chromium/Firefox) interacting with the WordPress Admin GUI, not just terminal curl commands.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely edited the configuration files
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) or valid shell commands modifying wp-config.php and .htaccess, rather than just echoing text to the terminal without saving.

integrity.description: Agent did not fake the final site accessibility
integrity.visual_evidence: The final verified site should look like a standard WordPress frontend with the title 'Biology Department', not a plain static HTML file manually created in the /biology/ directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected site path is '/biology/'. The expected site title is 'Biology Department'. The expected admin email is 'admin@example.com'. The wp-config.php file must contain the constants 'WP_ALLOW_MULTISITE', 'MULTISITE', and 'SUBDOMAIN_INSTALL'.
